r2), Abs(c1 - c2)) }\n\n// A valid single king move: to an adjacent cell (8 directions) within an n×n board\nghost predicate ValidKingStep(r1: int, c1: int, r2: int, c2: int, n: int)\n{\n  1 <= r1 <= n && 1 <= c1 <= n &&\n  1 <= r2 <= n && 1 <= c2 <= n &&\n  Abs(r1 - r2) <= 1 && Abs(c1 - c2) <= 1 &&\n  (r1 != r2 || c1 != c2)\n}\n\n// Greedy one step toward (tr, tc): move each coordinate ±1 in the correct direction\nghost function StepToward(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int): (int, int)\n{\n  (r + (if tr > r then 1 else if tr < r then -1 else 0),\n   c + (if tc > c then 1 else if tc < c then -1 else 0))\n}\n\n// EXISTENCE witness: the greedy step is a valid king move that reduces the\n// Chebyshev distance by exactly 1. Repeated application (induction on distance)\n// constructs a valid king path of length ChebyshevDist.\nghost predicate GreedyStepValid(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int, n: int)\n{\n  1 <= r <= n && 1 <= c <= n && 1 <= tr <= n && 1 <= tc <= n &&\n  ((r == tr && c == tc)\n   ||\n   (var p := StepToward(r, c, tr, tc);\n    ValidKingStep(r, c, p.0, p.1, n) &&\n    ChebyshevDist(p.0, p.1, tr, tc) == ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1))\n}\n\n// OPTIMALITY: no single king step can reduce Chebyshev distance by more than 1.\n// By induction on path length, any path of k steps reduces distance by at most k,\n// so the minimum number of moves from (r,c) to (tr,tc) is at least ChebyshevDist.\nghost predicate NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int)\n{\n  forall dr | -1 <= dr <= 1 ::\n    forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1 ::\n      ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n}\n\n// Turn structure: White takes turns 1, 3, 5, … ; Black takes turns 2, 4, 6, …\n// A king needing d optimal moves arrives at global turn:\n//   2d−1 if White (moves first), or 2d if Black (moves second).\n// If d = 0 the king starts on the coin (arrival turn 0).\nghost function ArrivalTurn(dist: int, movesFirst: bool): int\n{\n  if dist == 0 then 0\n  else if movesFirst then 2 * dist - 1\n  else 2 * dist\n}\n\n// The correct winner is the king whose arrival turn is strictly earlier.\nghost predicate CorrectWinner(winner: string, n: int, x: int, y: int)\n  requires n >= 2 && 1 <= x <= n && 1 <= y <= n\n{\n  var whiteDist := ChebyshevDist(1, 1, x, y);\n  var blackDist := ChebyshevDist(n, n, x, y);\n  var whiteTurn := ArrivalTurn(whiteDist, true);\n  var blackTurn := ArrivalTurn(blackDist, false);\n  (winner == \"White\" <==> whiteTurn <= blackTurn) &&\n  (winner == \"Black\" <==> whiteTurn > blackTurn)\n}\n\n// ── Lemmas ──\n\nlemma AbsTriangle(a: int, d: int)\n  requires -1 <= d <= 1\n  ensures Abs(a + d) >= Abs(a) - 1\n{\n}\n\nlemma NoStepReducesLemma(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(r, c, tr, tc)\n{\n  forall dr | -1 <= dr <= 1\n    ensures forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1 ::\n      ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n  {\n    forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1\n      ensures ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n    {\n      AbsTriangle(r - tr, dr);\n      AbsTriangle(c - tc, dc);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nlemma GreedyStepValidLemma(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int, n: int)\n  requires 1 <= r <= n && 1 <= c <= n && 1 <= tr <= n && 1 <= tc <= n\n  ensures GreedyStepValid(r, c, tr, tc, n)\n{\n  if r != tr || c != tc {\n    var p := StepToward(r, c, tr, tc);\n    assert 1 <= p.0 <= n;\n    assert 1 <= p.1 <= n;\n  }\n}\n\n// ── Method with specification ──\n\nmethod KingsRace(n: int, x: int, y: int) returns (winner: string)\n  requires n >= 2 && 1 <= x <= n && 1 <= y <= n\n  ensures CorrectWinner(winner, n, x, y)\n  ensures GreedyStepValid(1, 1, x, y, n)\n  ensures GreedyStepValid(n, n, x, y, n)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(1, 1, x, y)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(n, n, x, y)\n{\n  var dx1 := if x - 1 >= 0 then x - 1 else 1 - x;\n  var dy1 := if y - 1 >= 0 then y - 1 else 1 - y;\n  var dist1 := if dx1 >= dy1 then dx1 else dy1;\n\n  var dx2 := if x - n >= 0 then x - n else n - x;\n  var dy2 := if y - n >= 0 then y - n else n - y;\n  var dist2 := if dx2 >= dy2 then dx2 else dy2;\n\n  assert dist1 == ChebyshevDist(1, 1, x, y);\n  assert dist2 == ChebyshevDist(n, n, x, y);\n\n  GreedyStepValidLemma(1, 1, x, y, n);\n  GreedyStepValidLemma(n, n, x, y, n);\n  NoStepReducesLemma(1, 1, x, y);\n  NoStepReducesLemma(n, n, x, y);\n\n  if dist1 <= dist2 {\n    winner := \"White\";\n  } else {\n    winner := \"Black\";\n  }\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":11188,"cache_read_input_tokens":8610,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":11188},"output_tokens":10,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2e70972b-aa2e-4107-b01b-a3c6da36ef31","uuid":"b12f365a-6228-4dc9-bab3-c1fe5a5bab0f"},{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01THtBqFroqpADnP85faGr2t","type":"tool_result","content":"File created successfully at: /Users/mchiesa/work/projects/2026-02-dafny-liftings/smt_analysis/results/0014_1075_A/verified.dfy"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2e70972b-aa2e-4107-b01b-a3c6da36ef31","uuid":"34a22092-bd20-42d0-b50b-8686b52d507f","timestamp":"2026-03-17T19:06:44.403Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"create","filePath":"/Users/mchiesa/work/projects/2026-02-dafny-liftings/smt_analysis/results/0014_1075_A/verified.dfy","content":"// ── Helper functions ──\n\nghost function Abs(x: int): int\n{ if x >= 0 then x else -x }\n\nghost function Max(a: int, b: int): int\n{ if a >= b then a else b }\n\n// ── Problem formalization ──\n\n// Chebyshev (L∞) distance between two cells\nghost function ChebyshevDist(r1: int, c1: int, r2: int, c2: int): int\n{ Max(Abs(r1 - r2), Abs(c1 - c2)) }\n\n// A valid single king move: to an adjacent cell (8 directions) within an n×n board\nghost predicate ValidKingStep(r1: int, c1: int, r2: int, c2: int, n: int)\n{\n  1 <= r1 <= n && 1 <= c1 <= n &&\n  1 <= r2 <= n && 1 <= c2 <= n &&\n  Abs(r1 - r2) <= 1 && Abs(c1 - c2) <= 1 &&\n  (r1 != r2 || c1 != c2)\n}\n\n// Greedy one step toward (tr, tc): move each coordinate ±1 in the correct direction\nghost function StepToward(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int): (int, int)\n{\n  (r + (if tr > r then 1 else if tr < r then -1 else 0),\n   c + (if tc > c then 1 else if tc < c then -1 else 0))\n}\n\n// EXISTENCE witness: the greedy step is a valid king move that reduces the\n// Chebyshev distance by exactly 1. Repeated application (induction on distance)\n// constructs a valid king path of length ChebyshevDist.\nghost predicate GreedyStepValid(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int, n: int)\n{\n  1 <= r <= n && 1 <= c <= n && 1 <= tr <= n && 1 <= tc <= n &&\n  ((r == tr && c == tc)\n   ||\n   (var p := StepToward(r, c, tr, tc);\n    ValidKingStep(r, c, p.0, p.1, n) &&\n    ChebyshevDist(p.0, p.1, tr, tc) == ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1))\n}\n\n// OPTIMALITY: no single king step can reduce Chebyshev distance by more than 1.\n// By induction on path length, any path of k steps reduces distance by at most k,\n// so the minimum number of moves from (r,c) to (tr,tc) is at least ChebyshevDist.\nghost predicate NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int)\n{\n  forall dr | -1 <= dr <= 1 ::\n    forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1 ::\n      ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n}\n\n// Turn structure: White takes turns 1, 3, 5, … ; Black takes turns 2, 4, 6, …\n// A king needing d optimal moves arrives at global turn:\n//   2d−1 if White (moves first), or 2d if Black (moves second).\n// If d = 0 the king starts on the coin (arrival turn 0).\nghost function ArrivalTurn(dist: int, movesFirst: bool): int\n{\n  if dist == 0 then 0\n  else if movesFirst then 2 * dist - 1\n  else 2 * dist\n}\n\n// The correct winner is the king whose arrival turn is strictly earlier.\nghost predicate CorrectWinner(winner: string, n: int, x: int, y: int)\n  requires n >= 2 && 1 <= x <= n && 1 <= y <= n\n{\n  var whiteDist := ChebyshevDist(1, 1, x, y);\n  var blackDist := ChebyshevDist(n, n, x, y);\n  var whiteTurn := ArrivalTurn(whiteDist, true);\n  var blackTurn := ArrivalTurn(blackDist, false);\n  (winner == \"White\" <==> whiteTurn <= blackTurn) &&\n  (winner == \"Black\" <==> whiteTurn > blackTurn)\n}\n\n// ── Lemmas ──\n\nlemma AbsTriangle(a: int, d: int)\n  requires -1 <= d <= 1\n  ensures Abs(a + d) >= Abs(a) - 1\n{\n}\n\nlemma NoStepReducesLemma(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(r, c, tr, tc)\n{\n  forall dr | -1 <= dr <= 1\n    ensures forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1 ::\n      ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n  {\n    forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1\n      ensures ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n    {\n      AbsTriangle(r - tr, dr);\n      AbsTriangle(c - tc, dc);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nlemma GreedyStepValidLemma(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int, n: int)\n  requires 1 <= r <= n && 1 <= c <= n && 1 <= tr <= n && 1 <= tc <= n\n  ensures GreedyStepValid(r, c, tr, tc, n)\n{\n  if r != tr || c != tc {\n    var p := StepToward(r, c, tr, tc);\n    assert 1 <= p.0 <= n;\n    assert 1 <= p.1 <= n;\n  }\n}\n\n// ── Method with specification ──\n\nmethod KingsRace(n: int, x: int, y: int) returns (winner: string)\n  requires n >= 2 && 1 <= x <= n && 1 <= y <= n\n  ensures CorrectWinner(winner, n, x, y)\n  ensures GreedyStepValid(1, 1, x, y, n)\n  ensures GreedyStepValid(n, n, x, y, n)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(1, 1, x, y)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(n, n, x, y)\n{\n  var dx1 := if x - 1 >= 0 then x - 1 else 1 - x;\n  var dy1 := if y - 1 >= 0 then y - 1 else 1 - y;\n  var dist1 := if dx1 >= dy1 then dx1 else dy1;\n\n  var dx2 := if x - n >= 0 then x - n else n - x;\n  var dy2 := if y - n >= 0 then y - n else n - y;\n  var dist2 := if dx2 >= dy2 then dx2 else dy2;\n\n  assert dist1 == ChebyshevDist(1, 1, x, y);\n  assert dist2 == ChebyshevDist(n, n, x, y);\n\n  GreedyStepValidLemma(1, 1, x, y, n);\n  GreedyStepValidLemma(n, n, x, y, n);\n  NoStepReducesLemma(1, 1, x, y);\n  NoStepReducesLemma(n, n, x, y);\n\n  if dist1 <= dist2 {\n    winner := \"White\";\n  } else {\n    winner := \"Black\";\n  }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}},{"type":"rate_limit_event","rate_limit_info":{"status":"allowed","resetsAt":1773788400,"rateLimitType":"five_hour","overageStatus":"rejected","overageDisabledReason":"org_level_disabled","isUsingOverage":false},"uuid":"cadbc626-9dc2-424a-b0a3-7654a3392b29","session_id":"2e70972b-aa2e-4107-b01b-a3c6da36ef31"},{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01KjX9pVWAw1yxnN5mqR337p","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01P3Hp1qWFdEH7oaFs1HDVWq","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"/opt/homebrew/Cellar/dotnet@8/8.0.124/libexec/dotnet 1\n{\n}\n\nlemma MaxMonotone(a: int, b: int, c: int, d: int)\n  requires a >= c - 1\n  requires b >= d - 1\n  ensures Max(a, b) >= Max(c, d) - 1\n{\n}\n\nlemma NoStepReducesLemma(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(r, c, tr, tc)\n{\n  forall dr | -1 <= dr <= 1\n    ensures forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1 ::\n      ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n  {\n    forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1\n      ensures ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n    {\n      AbsTriangle(r - tr, dr);\n      AbsTriangle(c - tc, dc);\n      MaxMonotone(Abs(r + dr - tr), Abs(c + dc - tc), Abs(r - tr), Abs(c - tc));\n    }\n  }\n}","originalFile":"// ── Helper functions ──\n\nghost function Abs(x: int): int\n{ if x >= 0 then x else -x }\n\nghost function Max(a: int, b: int): int\n{ if a >= b then a else b }\n\n// ── Problem formalization ──\n\n// Chebyshev (L∞) distance between two cells\nghost function ChebyshevDist(r1: int, c1: int, r2: int, c2: int): int\n{ Max(Abs(r1 - r2), Abs(c1 - c2)) }\n\n// A valid single king move: to an adjacent cell (8 directions) within an n×n board\nghost predicate ValidKingStep(r1: int, c1: int, r2: int, c2: int, n: int)\n{\n  1 <= r1 <= n && 1 <= c1 <= n &&\n  1 <= r2 <= n && 1 <= c2 <= n &&\n  Abs(r1 - r2) <= 1 && Abs(c1 - c2) <= 1 &&\n  (r1 != r2 || c1 != c2)\n}\n\n// Greedy one step toward (tr, tc): move each coordinate ±1 in the correct direction\nghost function StepToward(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int): (int, int)\n{\n  (r + (if tr > r then 1 else if tr < r then -1 else 0),\n   c + (if tc > c then 1 else if tc < c then -1 else 0))\n}\n\n// EXISTENCE witness: the greedy step is a valid king move that reduces the\n// Chebyshev distance by exactly 1. Repeated application (induction on distance)\n// constructs a valid king path of length ChebyshevDist.\nghost predicate GreedyStepValid(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int, n: int)\n{\n  1 <= r <= n && 1 <= c <= n && 1 <= tr <= n && 1 <= tc <= n &&\n  ((r == tr && c == tc)\n   ||\n   (var p := StepToward(r, c, tr, tc);\n    ValidKingStep(r, c, p.0, p.1, n) &&\n    ChebyshevDist(p.0, p.1, tr, tc) == ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1))\n}\n\n// OPTIMALITY: no single king step can reduce Chebyshev distance by more than 1.\n// By induction on path length, any path of k steps reduces distance by at most k,\n// so the minimum number of moves from (r,c) to (tr,tc) is at least ChebyshevDist.\nghost predicate NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int)\n{\n  forall dr | -1 <= dr <= 1 ::\n    forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1 ::\n      ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n}\n\n// Turn structure: White takes turns 1, 3, 5, … ; Black takes turns 2, 4, 6, …\n// A king needing d optimal moves arrives at global turn:\n//   2d−1 if White (moves first), or 2d if Black (moves second).\n// If d = 0 the king starts on the coin (arrival turn 0).\nghost function ArrivalTurn(dist: int, movesFirst: bool): int\n{\n  if dist == 0 then 0\n  else if movesFirst then 2 * dist - 1\n  else 2 * dist\n}\n\n// The correct winner is the king whose arrival turn is strictly earlier.\nghost predicate CorrectWinner(winner: string, n: int, x: int, y: int)\n  requires n >= 2 && 1 <= x <= n && 1 <= y <= n\n{\n  var whiteDist := ChebyshevDist(1, 1, x, y);\n  var blackDist := ChebyshevDist(n, n, x, y);\n  var whiteTurn := ArrivalTurn(whiteDist, true);\n  var blackTurn := ArrivalTurn(blackDist, false);\n  (winner == \"White\" <==> whiteTurn <= blackTurn) &&\n  (winner == \"Black\" <==> whiteTurn > blackTurn)\n}\n\n// ── Lemmas ──\n\nlemma AbsTriangle(a: int, d: int)\n  requires -1 <= d <= 1\n  ensures Abs(a + d) >= Abs(a) - 1\n{\n}\n\nlemma NoStepReducesLemma(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(r, c, tr, tc)\n{\n  forall dr | -1 <= dr <= 1\n    ensures forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1 ::\n      ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n  {\n    forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1\n      ensures ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n    {\n      AbsTriangle(r - tr, dr);\n      AbsTriangle(c - tc, dc);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nlemma GreedyStepValidLemma(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int, n: int)\n  requires 1 <= r <= n && 1 <= c <= n && 1 <= tr <= n && 1 <= tc <= n\n  ensures GreedyStepValid(r, c, tr, tc, n)\n{\n  if r != tr || c != tc {\n    var p := StepToward(r, c, tr, tc);\n    assert 1 <= p.0 <= n;\n    assert 1 <= p.1 <= n;\n  }\n}\n\n// ── Method with specification ──\n\nmethod KingsRace(n: int, x: int, y: int) returns (winner: string)\n  requires n >= 2 && 1 <= x <= n && 1 <= y <= n\n  ensures CorrectWinner(winner, n, x, y)\n  ensures GreedyStepValid(1, 1, x, y, n)\n  ensures GreedyStepValid(n, n, x, y, n)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(1, 1, x, y)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(n, n, x, y)\n{\n  var dx1 := if x - 1 >= 0 then x - 1 else 1 - x;\n  var dy1 := if y - 1 >= 0 then y - 1 else 1 - y;\n  var dist1 := if dx1 >= dy1 then dx1 else dy1;\n\n  var dx2 := if x - n >= 0 then x - n else n - x;\n  var dy2 := if y - n >= 0 then y - n else n - y;\n  var dist2 := if dx2 >= dy2 then dx2 else dy2;\n\n  assert dist1 == ChebyshevDist(1, 1, x, y);\n  assert dist2 == ChebyshevDist(n, n, x, y);\n\n  GreedyStepValidLemma(1, 1, x, y, n);\n  GreedyStepValidLemma(n, n, x, y, n);\n  NoStepReducesLemma(1, 1, x, y);\n  NoStepReducesLemma(n, n, x, y);\n\n  if dist1 <= dist2 {\n    winner := \"White\";\n  } else {\n    winner := \"Black\";\n  }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":82,"oldLines":6,"newStart":82,"newLines":13,"lines":[" {"," }"," ","+lemma MaxMonotone(a: int, b: int, c: int, d: int)","+  requires a >= c - r2), Abs(c1 - c2)) }\n\n// A valid single king move: to an adjacent cell (8 directions) within an n×n board\nghost predicate ValidKingStep(r1: int, c1: int, r2: int, c2: int, n: int)\n{\n  1 <= r1 <= n && 1 <= c1 <= n &&\n  1 <= r2 <= n && 1 <= c2 <= n &&\n  Abs(r1 - r2) <= 1 && Abs(c1 - c2) <= 1 &&\n  (r1 != r2 || c1 != c2)\n}\n\n// Greedy one step toward (tr, tc): move each coordinate ±1 in the correct direction\nghost function StepToward(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int): (int, int)\n{\n  (r + (if tr > r then 1 else if tr < r then -1 else 0),\n   c + (if tc > c then 1 else if tc < c then -1 else 0))\n}\n\n// EXISTENCE witness: the greedy step is a valid king move that reduces the\n// Chebyshev distance by exactly 1. Repeated application (induction on distance)\n// constructs a valid king path of length ChebyshevDist.\nghost predicate GreedyStepValid(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int, n: int)\n{\n  1 <= r <= n && 1 <= c <= n && 1 <= tr <= n && 1 <= tc <= n &&\n  ((r == tr && c == tc)\n   ||\n   (var p := StepToward(r, c, tr, tc);\n    ValidKingStep(r, c, p.0, p.1, n) &&\n    ChebyshevDist(p.0, p.1, tr, tc) == ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1))\n}\n\n// OPTIMALITY: no single king step can reduce Chebyshev distance by more than 1.\n// By induction on path length, any path of k steps reduces distance by at most k,\n// so the minimum number of moves from (r,c) to (tr,tc) is at least ChebyshevDist.\nghost predicate NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int)\n{\n  forall dr | -1 <= dr <= 1 ::\n    forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1 ::\n      ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n}\n\n// Turn structure: White takes turns 1, 3, 5, … ; Black takes turns 2, 4, 6, …\n// A king needing d optimal moves arrives at global turn:\n//   2d−1 if White (moves first), or 2d if Black (moves second).\n// If d = 0 the king starts on the coin (arrival turn 0).\nghost function ArrivalTurn(dist: int, movesFirst: bool): int\n{\n  if dist == 0 then 0\n  else if movesFirst then 2 * dist - 1\n  else 2 * dist\n}\n\n// The correct winner is the king whose arrival turn is strictly earlier.\nghost predicate CorrectWinner(winner: string, n: int, x: int, y: int)\n  requires n >= 2 && 1 <= x <= n && 1 <= y <= n\n{\n  var whiteDist := ChebyshevDist(1, 1, x, y);\n  var blackDist := ChebyshevDist(n, n, x, y);\n  var whiteTurn := ArrivalTurn(whiteDist, true);\n  var blackTurn := ArrivalTurn(blackDist, false);\n  (winner == \"White\" <==> whiteTurn <= blackTurn) &&\n  (winner == \"Black\" <==> whiteTurn > blackTurn)\n}\n\n// ── Lemmas ──\n\nlemma AbsTriangle(a: int, d: int)\n  requires -1 <= d <= 1\n  ensures Abs(a + d) >= Abs(a) - 1\n{\n}\n\nlemma MaxMonotone(a: int, b: int, c: int, d: int)\n  requires a >= c - 1\n  requires b >= d - 1\n  ensures Max(a, b) >= Max(c, d) - 1\n{\n}\n\nlemma NoStepReducesLemma(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(r, c, tr, tc)\n{\n  forall dr | -1 <= dr <= 1\n    ensures forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1 ::\n      ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n  {\n    forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1\n      ensures ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n    {\n      AbsTriangle(r - tr, dr);\n      AbsTriangle(c - tc, dc);\n      MaxMonotone(Abs(r + dr - tr), Abs(c + dc - tc), Abs(r - tr), Abs(c - tc));\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nlemma GreedyStepValidLemma(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int, n: int)\n  requires 1 <= r <= n && 1 <= c <= n && 1 <= tr <= n && 1 <= tc <= n\n  ensures GreedyStepValid(r, c, tr, tc, n)\n{\n  if r != tr || c != tc {\n    var p := StepToward(r, c, tr, tc);\n    assert 1 <= p.0 <= n;\n    assert 1 <= p.1 <= n;\n  }\n}\n\n// ── Method with specification ──\n\nmethod KingsRace(n: int, x: int, y: int) returns (winner: string)\n  requires n >= 2 && 1 <= x <= n && 1 <= y <= n\n  ensures CorrectWinner(winner, n, x, y)\n  ensures GreedyStepValid(1, 1, x, y, n)\n  ensures GreedyStepValid(n, n, x, y, n)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(1, 1, x, y)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(n, n, x, y)\n{\n  var dx1 := if x - 1 >= 0 then x - 1 else 1 - x;\n  var dy1 := if y - 1 >= 0 then y - 1 else 1 - y;\n  var dist1 := if dx1 >= dy1 then dx1 else dy1;\n\n  var dx2 := if x - n >= 0 then x - n else n - x;\n  var dy2 := if y - n >= 0 then y - n else n - y;\n  var dist2 := if dx2 >= dy2 then dx2 else dy2;\n\n  assert dist1 == ChebyshevDist(1, 1, x, y);\n  assert dist2 == ChebyshevDist(n, n, x, y);\n\n  GreedyStepValidLemma(1, 1, x, y, n);\n  GreedyStepValidLemma(n, n, x, y, n);\n  NoStepReducesLemma(1, 1, x, y);\n  NoStepReducesLemma(n, n, x, y);\n\n  if dist1 <= dist2 {\n    winner := \"White\";\n  } else {\n    winner := \"Black\";\n  }\n}\n","numLines":152,"startLine":1,"totalLines":152}}},{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01JmeXBrBTwGnBg26b9iCKRi","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Let me try a different approach for the NoStepReducesLemma. n >= 0 then y - n else n - y;\n  var dist2 := if dx2 >= dy2 then dx2 else dy2;\n\n  assert dist1 == ChebyshevDist(1, 1, x, y);\n  assert dist2 == ChebyshevDist(n, n, x, y);\n\n  GreedyStepValidLemma(1, 1, x, y, n);\n  GreedyStepValidLemma(n, n, x, y, n);\n  NoStepReducesLemma(1, 1, x, y);\n  NoStepReducesLemma(n, n, x, y);\n\n  if dist1 <= dist2 {\n    winner := \"White\";\n  } else {\n    winner := \"Black\";\n  }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":89,"oldLines":6,"newStart":89,"newLines":19,"lines":[" {"," }"," ","+lemma NoStepReducesSingle(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int, dr: int, dc: int)","+  requires -1 <= dr <= 1 && -1 <= dc <= 1","+  ensures ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n  {\n    NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, dr, dc);\n  }\n}","originalFile":"// ── Helper functions ──\n\nghost function Abs(x: int): int\n{ if x >= 0 then x else -x }\n\nghost function Max(a: int, b: int): int\n{ if a >= b then a else b }\n\n// ── Problem formalization ──\n\n// Chebyshev (L∞) distance between two cells\nghost function ChebyshevDist(r1: int, c1: int, r2: int, c2: int): int\n{ Max(Abs(r1 - r2), Abs(c1 - c2)) }\n\n// A valid single king move: to an adjacent cell (8 directions) within an n×n board\nghost predicate ValidKingStep(r1: int, c1: int, r2: int, c2: int, n: int)\n{\n  1 <= r1 <= n && 1 <= c1 <= n &&\n  1 <= r2 <= n && 1 <= c2 <= n &&\n  Abs(r1 - r2) <= 1 && Abs(c1 - c2) <= 1 &&\n  (r1 != r2 || c1 != c2)\n}\n\n// Greedy one step toward (tr, tc): move each coordinate ±1 in the correct direction\nghost function StepToward(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int): (int, int)\n{\n  (r + (if tr > r then 1 else if tr < r then -1 else 0),\n   c + (if tc > c then 1 else if tc < c then -1 else 0))\n}\n\n// EXISTENCE witness: the greedy step is a valid king move that reduces the\n// Chebyshev distance by exactly 1. Repeated application (induction on distance)\n// constructs a valid king path of length ChebyshevDist.\nghost predicate GreedyStepValid(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int, n: int)\n{\n  1 <= r <= n && 1 <= c <= n && 1 <= tr <= n && 1 <= tc <= n &&\n  ((r == tr && c == tc)\n   ||\n   (var p := StepToward(r, c, tr, tc);\n    ValidKingStep(r, c, p.0, p.1, n) &&\n    ChebyshevDist(p.0, p.1, tr, tc) == ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1))\n}\n\n// OPTIMALITY: no single king step can reduce Chebyshev distance by more than 1.\n// By induction on path length, any path of k steps reduces distance by at most k,\n// so the minimum number of moves from (r,c) to (tr,tc) is at least ChebyshevDist.\nghost predicate NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int)\n{\n  forall dr | -1 <= dr <= 1 ::\n    forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1 ::\n      ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n}\n\n// Turn structure: White takes turns 1, 3, 5, … ; Black takes turns 2, 4, 6, …\n// A king needing d optimal moves arrives at global turn:\n//   2d−1 if White (moves first), or 2d if Black (moves second).\n// If d = 0 the king starts on the coin (arrival turn 0).\nghost function ArrivalTurn(dist: int, movesFirst: bool): int\n{\n  if dist == 0 then 0\n  else if movesFirst then 2 * dist - 1\n  else 2 * dist\n}\n\n// The correct winner is the king whose arrival turn is strictly earlier.\nghost predicate CorrectWinner(winner: string, n: int, x: int, y: int)\n  requires n >= 2 && 1 <= x <= n && 1 <= y <= n\n{\n  var whiteDist := ChebyshevDist(1, 1, x, y);\n  var blackDist := ChebyshevDist(n, n, x, y);\n  var whiteTurn := ArrivalTurn(whiteDist, true);\n  var blackTurn := ArrivalTurn(blackDist, false);\n  (winner == \"White\" <==> whiteTurn <= blackTurn) &&\n  (winner == \"Black\" <==> whiteTurn > blackTurn)\n}\n\n// ── Lemmas ──\n\nlemma AbsTriangle(a: int, d: int)\n  requires -1 <= d <= 1\n  ensures Abs(a + d) >= Abs(a) - 1\n{\n}\n\nlemma MaxMonotone(a: int, b: int, c: int, d: int)\n  requires a >= c - 1\n  requires b >= d - 1\n  ensures Max(a, b) >= Max(c, d) - 1\n{\n}\n\nlemma NoStepReducesSingle(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int, dr: int, dc: int)\n  requires -1 <= dr <= 1 && -1 <= dc <= 1\n  ensures ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n{\n  var a := r - tr;\n  var b := c - tc;\n  AbsTriangle(a, dr);\n  AbsTriangle(b, dc);\n  assert r + dr - tr == a + dr;\n  assert c + dc - tc == b + dc;\n  MaxMonotone(Abs(a + dr), Abs(b + dc), Abs(a), Abs(b));\n}\n\nlemma NoStepReducesLemma(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(r, c, tr, tc)\n{\n  forall dr | -1 <= dr <= 1\n    ensures forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1 ::\n      ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n  {\n    forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1\n      ensures ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n    {\n      NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, dr, dc);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nlemma GreedyStepValidLemma(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int, n: int)\n  requires 1 <= r <= n && 1 <= c <= n && 1 <= tr <= n && 1 <= tc <= n\n  ensures GreedyStepValid(r, c, tr, tc, n)\n{\n  if r != tr || c != tc {\n    var p := StepToward(r, c, tr, tc);\n    assert 1 <= p.0 <= n;\n    assert 1 <= p.1 <= n;\n  }\n}\n\n// ── Method with specification ──\n\nmethod KingsRace(n: int, x: int, y: int) returns (winner: string)\n  requires n >= 2 && 1 <= x <= n && 1 <= y <= n\n  ensures CorrectWinner(winner, n, x, y)\n  ensures GreedyStepValid(1, 1, x, y, n)\n  ensures GreedyStepValid(n, n, x, y, n)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(1, 1, x, y)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(n, n, x, y)\n{\n  var dx1 := if x - 1 >= 0 then x - 1 else 1 - x;\n  var dy1 := if y - 1 >= 0 then y - 1 else 1 - y;\n  var dist1 := if dx1 >= dy1 then dx1 else dy1;\n\n  var dx2 := if x - n >= 0 then x - n else n - x;\n  var dy2 := if y - n >= 0 then y - n else n - y;\n  var dist2 := if dx2 >= dy2 then dx2 else dy2;\n\n  assert dist1 == ChebyshevDist(1, 1, x, y);\n  assert dist2 == ChebyshevDist(n, n, x, y);\n\n  GreedyStepValidLemma(1, 1, x, y, n);\n  GreedyStepValidLemma(n, n, x, y, n);\n  NoStepReducesLemma(1, 1, x, y);\n  NoStepReducesLemma(n, n, x, y);\n\n  if dist1 <= dist2 {\n    winner := \"White\";\n  } else {\n    winner := \"Black\";\n  }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":105,"oldLines":15,"newStart":105,"newLines":10,"lines":[" lemma NoStepReducesLemma(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int)","   ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(r, c, tr, tc)"," {","-  forall dr | -1 <= dr <= 1","-    ensures forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1 ::","-      ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n  {\n    NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, dr, dc);\n  }\n}","newString":"lemma NoStepReducesLemma(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(r, c, tr, tc)\n{\n  NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, -1, -1);\n  NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, -1, 0);\n  NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, -1, 1);\n  NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, 0, -1);\n  NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, 0, 0);\n  NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, 0, 1);\n  NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, 1, -1);\n  NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, 1, 0);\n  NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, 1, 1);\n}","originalFile":"// ── Helper functions ──\n\nghost function Abs(x: int): int\n{ if x >= 0 then x else -x }\n\nghost function Max(a: int, b: int): int\n{ if a >= b then a else b }\n\n// ── Problem formalization ──\n\n// Chebyshev (L∞) distance between two cells\nghost function ChebyshevDist(r1: int, c1: int, r2: int, c2: int): int\n{ Max(Abs(r1 - r2), Abs(c1 - c2)) }\n\n// A valid single king move: to an adjacent cell (8 directions) within an n×n board\nghost predicate ValidKingStep(r1: int, c1: int, r2: int, c2: int, n: int)\n{\n  1 <= r1 <= n && 1 <= c1 <= n &&\n  1 <= r2 <= n && 1 <= c2 <= n &&\n  Abs(r1 - r2) <= 1 && Abs(c1 - c2) <= 1 &&\n  (r1 != r2 || c1 != c2)\n}\n\n// Greedy one step toward (tr, tc): move each coordinate ±1 in the correct direction\nghost function StepToward(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int): (int, int)\n{\n  (r + (if tr > r then 1 else if tr < r then -1 else 0),\n   c + (if tc > c then 1 else if tc < c then -1 else 0))\n}\n\n// EXISTENCE witness: the greedy step is a valid king move that reduces the\n// Chebyshev distance by exactly 1. Repeated application (induction on distance)\n// constructs a valid king path of length ChebyshevDist.\nghost predicate GreedyStepValid(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int, n: int)\n{\n  1 <= r <= n && 1 <= c <= n && 1 <= tr <= n && 1 <= tc <= n &&\n  ((r == tr && c == tc)\n   ||\n   (var p := StepToward(r, c, tr, tc);\n    ValidKingStep(r, c, p.0, p.1, n) &&\n    ChebyshevDist(p.0, p.1, tr, tc) == ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1))\n}\n\n// OPTIMALITY: no single king step can reduce Chebyshev distance by more than 1.\n// By induction on path length, any path of k steps reduces distance by at most k,\n// so the minimum number of moves from (r,c) to (tr,tc) is at least ChebyshevDist.\nghost predicate NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int)\n{\n  forall dr | -1 <= dr <= 1 ::\n    forall dc | -1 <= dc <= 1 ::\n      ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n}\n\n// Turn structure: White takes turns 1, 3, 5, … ; Black takes turns 2, 4, 6, …\n// A king needing d optimal moves arrives at global turn:\n//   2d−1 if White (moves first), or 2d if Black (moves second).\n// If d = 0 the king starts on the coin (arrival turn 0).\nghost function ArrivalTurn(dist: int, movesFirst: bool): int\n{\n  if dist == 0 then 0\n  else if movesFirst then 2 * dist - 1\n  else 2 * dist\n}\n\n// The correct winner is the king whose arrival turn is strictly earlier.\nghost predicate CorrectWinner(winner: string, n: int, x: int, y: int)\n  requires n >= 2 && 1 <= x <= n && 1 <= y <= n\n{\n  var whiteDist := ChebyshevDist(1, 1, x, y);\n  var blackDist := ChebyshevDist(n, n, x, y);\n  var whiteTurn := ArrivalTurn(whiteDist, true);\n  var blackTurn := ArrivalTurn(blackDist, false);\n  (winner == \"White\" <==> whiteTurn <= blackTurn) &&\n  (winner == \"Black\" <==> whiteTurn > blackTurn)\n}\n\n// ── Lemmas ──\n\nlemma AbsTriangle(a: int, d: int)\n  requires -1 <= d <= 1\n  ensures Abs(a + d) >= Abs(a) - 1\n{\n}\n\nlemma MaxMonotone(a: int, b: int, c: int, d: int)\n  requires a >= c - 1\n  requires b >= d - 1\n  ensures Max(a, b) >= Max(c, d) - 1\n{\n}\n\nlemma NoStepReducesSingle(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int, dr: int, dc: int)\n  requires -1 <= dr <= 1 && -1 <= dc <= 1\n  ensures ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n{\n  var a := r - tr;\n  var b := c - tc;\n  AbsTriangle(a, dr);\n  AbsTriangle(b, dc);\n  assert r + dr - tr == a + dr;\n  assert c + dc - tc == b + dc;\n  MaxMonotone(Abs(a + dr), Abs(b + dc), Abs(a), Abs(b));\n}\n\nlemma NoStepReducesLemma(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(r, c, tr, tc)\n{\n  forall dr: int, dc: int | -1 <= dr <= 1 && -1 <= dc <= 1\n    ensures ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1\n  {\n    NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, dr, dc);\n  }\n}\n\nlemma GreedyStepValidLemma(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int, n: int)\n  requires 1 <= r <= n && 1 <= c <= n && 1 <= tr <= n && 1 <= tc <= n\n  ensures GreedyStepValid(r, c, tr, tc, n)\n{\n  if r != tr || c != tc {\n    var p := StepToward(r, c, tr, tc);\n    assert 1 <= p.0 <= n;\n    assert 1 <= p.1 <= n;\n  }\n}\n\n// ── Method with specification ──\n\nmethod KingsRace(n: int, x: int, y: int) returns (winner: string)\n  requires n >= 2 && 1 <= x <= n && 1 <= y <= n\n  ensures CorrectWinner(winner, n, x, y)\n  ensures GreedyStepValid(1, 1, x, y, n)\n  ensures GreedyStepValid(n, n, x, y, n)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(1, 1, x, y)\n  ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(n, n, x, y)\n{\n  var dx1 := if x - 1 >= 0 then x - 1 else 1 - x;\n  var dy1 := if y - 1 >= 0 then y - 1 else 1 - y;\n  var dist1 := if dx1 >= dy1 then dx1 else dy1;\n\n  var dx2 := if x - n >= 0 then x - n else n - x;\n  var dy2 := if y - n >= 0 then y - n else n - y;\n  var dist2 := if dx2 >= dy2 then dx2 else dy2;\n\n  assert dist1 == ChebyshevDist(1, 1, x, y);\n  assert dist2 == ChebyshevDist(n, n, x, y);\n\n  GreedyStepValidLemma(1, 1, x, y, n);\n  GreedyStepValidLemma(n, n, x, y, n);\n  NoStepReducesLemma(1, 1, x, y);\n  NoStepReducesLemma(n, n, x, y);\n\n  if dist1 <= dist2 {\n    winner := \"White\";\n  } else {\n    winner := \"Black\";\n  }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":105,"oldLines":11,"newStart":105,"newLines":15,"lines":[" lemma NoStepReducesLemma(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int)","   ensures NoStepReducesByMoreThanOne(r, c, tr, tc)"," {","-  forall dr: int, dc: int | -1 <= dr <= 1 && -1 <= dc <= 1","-    ensures ChebyshevDist(r + dr, c + dc, tr, tc) >= ChebyshevDist(r, c, tr, tc) - 1","-  {","-    NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, dr, dc);","-  }","+  NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, -1, -1);","+  NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, -1, 0);","+  NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, -1, 1);","+  NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, 0, -1);","+  NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, 0, 0);","+  NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, 0, 1);","+  NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, 1, -1);","+  NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, 1, 0);","+  NoStepReducesSingle(r, c, tr, tc, 1, 1);"," }"," "," lemma GreedyStepValidLemma(r: int, c: int, tr: int, tc: int, n: int)"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}},{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01Hv9RmFnkVGiTeU46Ncggof","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01VzMySGj3GtmiRJn96TxUJb","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"/opt/homebrew/Cellar/dotnet@8/8.0.124/libexec/dotnet /Users/mchiesa/work/projects/2026-02-dafny-liftings/dafny-source/Binaries/Dafny.dll verify /Users/mchiesa/work/projects/2026-02-dafny-liftings/smt_analysis/results/0014_1075_A/verified.dfy --verification-time-limit 60 2>&1","description":"Run Dafny verification (attempt 5)","timeout":120000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":11774,"cache_read_input_tokens":65926,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":11774},"output_tokens":26,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2e70972b-aa2e-4107-b01b-a3c6da36ef31","uuid":"6113d557-ba99-4bdb-81ce-1d462d7fdee6"},{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"tool_result","content":"Exit code 2\nverified.dfy(49,2): Warning: Could not find a trigger for this quantifier.
